resistant.headline: Textbook resistant
why: AMRFinderPlus: QRDR point mutations at gyrA (gyrA_D87N/gyrA_S83L), parC (parC_E84V/parC_S80I), parE (parE_I529L) -> spectrum-confirmed category-(i) evidence.
Split: heldout_group (never trained on). Verdict: likely to fail.

susceptible.headline: Honest likely-to-work
why: Lab phenotype: susceptible on all 5 modeled drugs (re-derived labels).
gyrA and parC loci verified wild-type-intact by the k-mer callability check — 'no mutation found' is a measured WT, not a missing locus.
Split: test. Verdict: likely to work on 5/5 drugs.

refusal.headline: The refusal
why: Held-out genetic group (clade 6); nearest training genome is 1.03 ANI-distance away.
Calibrated ciprofloxacin score 0.088 — a naive 0.5-threshold caller would have said "likely to work". Lab truth: Resistant. The abstention band catches exactly this error: no-call on 5/5 drugs.
This is the win, not a failure: abstention is standard behavior in every credible AMR tool (EUCAST ATU, ResFinder panel scope) — here it is automated and measured.
